DIP: The DIP (or Distal Interphalangeal) joint is located near the tip of the finger, between the last two phalanges. It appears as a small gap representing the joint space on the X-ray.
MCP: The MCP (or Metacarpophalangeal) joint is found at the knuckle, connecting fingers to the palm. It appears as a prominent gap near the base of each finger.
PIP: The PIP (or Proximal Interphalangeal) joint is located between the first and second phalanges, appearing as a clear gap between the finger bones. It is below the DIP and above the MCP.
Radius: The Radius is the thicker and shorter of the two forearm bones, located on the thumb side. It appears as a robust and continuous bone on the X-ray.
Ulna: The Ulna runs parallel to the Radius and is typically longer and thinner, extending to the elbow.
Wrist: The Wrist comprises multiple small carpal bones at the base of the hand, forming the connection to the forearm.
